creator: dch@freebsd.org
text:
a bit more context:

buildkite.com is a hosted service (like cirrus etc) that allows,
among many other things, to trigger builds based on github pull
requests, and run these in parallel or serial as required, on
agents that we run and host on our own infrastructure.

These build steps can do linting, style, or any other action,
and can be made to wait for a manual approval from a designated
committer, for example. It's also possible for approved users to 
submit a manual build request if needed.

The data sent from github -> buildkite is the typical public
github webhook information - the person submitting the commit
or PR, the branch or tag, and further public metadata.

Buildkite then uses a yaml file to decide what pipeline
should be invoked, typically it's one already present in the
branch, and whether a particular user is allowed to kick off
a build.

The builds run happily in jails, using a ported agent 
https://freshports.org/devel/buildkite-agent (dch as maintainer)
and has a set of tokens that each agent is assigned, thus allowing
people to contribute suitable hardware if they wish, or to use
freebsd cluster resources in a jail if thats an option in future.
I'm prepping some notes on doing this atm.

On build completion (or failure), logs & artifacts can be
optionally stored for later access.

There are further controls in the buildkite.com/freebsd org
that can be used to constrain who can trigger a build, ignoring
specific branches, etc.

I'm happy to elaborate on that elsewhere, and add users as
required as admins. currently thats skreuzer, dch, imp.
